text: God creates the heavens and the earth in six days, separating light from darkness, sky from water, land from sea, creating plants trees sun moon stars fish birds animals and finally mankind in his own image
God forms Adam from dust and breathes life into him, plants the Garden of Eden, creates Eve from Adam's rib as a companion and helper
The serpent tempts Eve and Adam to eat the forbidden fruit from the tree of knowledge of good and evil, they disobey God and are cast out of the Garden of Eden, the fall of man
Cain murders his brother Abel out of jealousy because God accepted Abel's offering but not Cain's, Cain becomes a wanderer marked by God
God sees the wickedness of mankind and sends a great flood to destroy the earth, commands Noah a righteous man to build an ark and save his family and two of every animal
The people build the Tower of Babel to reach heaven, God confuses their language and scatters them across the earth
God calls Abram to leave his country and family, promises to make him a great nation and bless all the families of the earth through him
God makes a covenant with Abraham and promises descendants as numerous as the stars, Abraham believes God and it is counted as righteousness
God tests Abraham by commanding him to sacrifice his son Isaac on Mount Moriah, Abraham obeys and just before he kills Isaac God provides a ram caught in a thicket as a substitute
Jacob sees a vision of a ladder or stairway reaching from earth to heaven with angels ascending and descending, God speaks and renews the covenant
Joseph is given a coat of many colors by his father Jacob who loves him more than his brothers, his brothers are jealous and sell him into slavery in Egypt
Joseph interprets Pharaoh's dreams about seven fat cows and seven thin cows as seven years of plenty and seven years of famine, is made second in command over Egypt
God speaks to Moses from a burning bush that is not consumed by fire, reveals his name I AM WHO I AM and calls Moses to deliver Israel out of Egypt
God sends ten plagues upon Egypt through Moses and Aaron to free the Israelites: blood, frogs, lice, flies, livestock disease, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and death of firstborn
God institutes the Passover feast, Israelites put lamb's blood on their doorposts so the angel of death will pass over their homes while the firstborn of Egypt are killed
God parts the Red Sea so the Israelites can cross on dry ground, the Egyptian army pursuing them is swallowed up by the returning waters
God gives Moses the Ten Commandments on Mount Sinai, the law of God including no other gods, no idols, honor the Sabbath, honor parents, no murder adultery stealing lying
God speaks through a donkey to rebuke the prophet Balaam when the donkey sees the angel of the Lord blocking the road that Balaam cannot see
God instructs Joshua to march the Israelite army around Jericho for seven days blowing trumpets, on the seventh day the walls of Jericho fall and they take the city
Delilah persuades Samson to reveal the secret of his strength which is his long hair, she cuts his hair while he sleeps and the Philistines capture and blind him
Ruth pledges loyalty to her mother-in-law Naomi after the death of their husbands, saying where you go I will go and where you die I will die, your God will be my God
tags: where you go I will go

text: The young shepherd David defeats the giant Philistine warrior Goliath using only a sling and a stone, Goliath had challenged the army of Israel for forty days
King David commits adultery with Bathsheba the wife of Uriah the Hittite and arranges for Uriah to be killed in battle to cover up his sin
The prophet Elijah challenges the four hundred and fifty prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel, God sends fire from heaven to consume Elijah's water-soaked sacrifice proving he is the true God
Elijah flees from Jezebel into the wilderness, becomes depressed and wants to die, an angel brings him food and water, God speaks to him in a still small voice
Naaman the Syrian army commander is healed of leprosy after he dips himself seven times in the Jordan River as commanded by the prophet Elisha
God allows Satan to test the righteous man Job by taking away his children wealth and health, yet Job does not curse God but worships him saying the Lord gives and the Lord takes away
The Lord is my shepherd I shall not want, he makes me lie down in green pastures beside still waters, restores my soul, walks with me through the valley of the shadow of death
My God my God why have you forsaken me, a psalm of David expressing deep suffering and abandonment yet ending in praise and trust
David's prayer of repentance after his sin with Bathsheba, asking God to create in me a clean heart and renew a right spirit within me, blot out my transgressions
He who dwells in the secret place of the Most High shall abide under the shadow of the Almighty, his angels will guard you and lift you up, no plague shall come near your dwelling
God knit me together in my mother's womb, I am fearfully and wonderfully made, God knows when I sit and rise and every word before I speak it, nowhere can I flee from his presence
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding, in all your ways acknowledge him and he will make your paths straight
They that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength, they shall mount up with wings as eagles, they shall run and not be weary, walk and not faint
The suffering servant is wounded for our transgressions bruised for our iniquities, by his stripes we are healed, he bore our sins like a lamb to the slaughter
God's thoughts are not our thoughts and his ways are not our ways, as the heavens are higher than the earth so are his ways higher than ours
God says I know the plans I have for you, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope
God shows Ezekiel a valley full of dry bones and commands him to prophesy to the bones, the bones come together with flesh and skin and breath of life enters them, a symbol of Israel's restoration
Shadrach Meshach and Abednego refuse to bow to King Nebuchadnezzar's golden idol and are thrown into the fiery furnace, a fourth figure like the Son of God is seen walking with them in the fire
Daniel prays to God three times a day despite a royal decree forbidding it, is thrown into the lion's den, God shuts the mouths of the lions and Daniel is unharmed
Jonah flees from God's call to preach to Nineveh, is swallowed by a great fish and spends three days and nights inside before being vomited onto dry land
He has shown you what is good, to do justly love mercy and walk humbly with your God
The birth of Jesus Christ, an angel appears to Joseph and tells him that Mary has conceived by the Holy Spirit and he shall call his name Jesus for he will save his people from their sins
Wise men from the East follow a star to find the baby Jesus and bring gifts of gold frankincense and myrrh, Herod tries to find and kill the child
Jesus is led by the Spirit into the wilderness and fasted forty days and nights, Satan tempts him three times with bread power and kingdoms but Jesus resists using scripture
The Sermon on the Mount begins with the Beatitudes: blessed are the poor in spirit meek merciful pure peacemakers persecuted, you are the salt of the earth and light of the world
Blessed are the peacemakers for they shall be called children of God
Jesus teaches his disciples how to pray with the Lord's Prayer: Our Father who is in heaven hallowed be your name your kingdom come your will be done on earth as in heaven
Seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness and all these things shall be added to you
Ask and it shall be given to you seek and you shall find knock and the door shall be opened to you
Peter walks on water toward Jesus during a storm, but when he looks at the waves he becomes afraid and begins to sink, Jesus stretches out his hand and saves him
Jesus tells the parable of the sheep and goats, those who fed the hungry clothed the naked and visited prisoners did it unto Jesus, those who did not are sent away
Jesus is risen from the dead, he appears to Mary Magdalene and the disciples and gives the Great Commission to go and make disciples of all nations baptizing and teaching
Jesus rebukes the wind and waves during a violent storm on the Sea of Galilee and they are completely calm, his disciples marvel and ask who is this that even the winds obey
The angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she will conceive and bear the Son of God by the Holy Spirit, Mary sings the Magnificat praising God
Jesus is born in Bethlehem in a manger because there was no room in the inn, shepherds are told by angels and go to see the baby Jesus
Jesus reads from Isaiah in the synagogue saying the Spirit of the Lord is upon me to preach good news to the poor free the captives heal the blind and proclaim the acceptable year of the Lord
Jesus tells the parable of the Good Samaritan to answer the question who is my neighbor, a Samaritan who was despised stops to help a man beaten and left for dead
Mary sits at Jesus's feet listening to him while her sister Martha is distracted with much serving, Jesus says Mary has chosen the better part
Jesus tells the parable of the Prodigal Son who takes his inheritance wastes it in riotous living comes to himself and returns home, his father sees him from afar runs and embraces him
Jesus tells the parable of the lost sheep where a shepherd leaves ninety nine sheep to search for the one that is lost, and rejoices when it is found
Zacchaeus the wealthy tax collector climbs a sycamore tree to see Jesus, Jesus calls him down and comes to his house, Zacchaeus repents and promises to give back what he stole
In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God and the Word was God, through him all things were made, and the Word became flesh and dwelt among us full of grace and truth
Jesus turns water into wine at a wedding in Cana, his first miracle, the master of the feast says the best wine has been saved for last
Jesus tells Nicodemus a Pharisee that he must be born again of water and the Spirit to enter the kingdom of God, God so loved the world that he gave his only Son
For God so loved the world that he gave his only begotten Son that whoever believes in him should not perish but have everlasting life
Jesus talks with the Samaritan woman at the well and offers her living water saying whoever drinks the water he gives will never thirst again, reveals he is the Messiah
Jesus feeds five thousand people with only five loaves of bread and two fish, the crowd eats and is satisfied and twelve baskets of leftover fragments are collected
Jesus says I have come that they may have life and have it more abundantly, he is the good shepherd who lays down his life for his sheep
tags: thief comes to steal kill destroy

text: Jesus raises his friend Lazarus from the dead after four days in the tomb, Jesus wept at the grave showing his love, declares I am the resurrection and the life
Jesus wept, the shortest verse in the Bible, Jesus cries at the tomb of Lazarus showing his compassion and love
Jesus washes his disciples' feet at the Last Supper teaching them to serve one another as he has served them, gives them a new commandment to love one another
Jesus comforts his disciples saying let not your hearts be troubled, believe in God believe also in me, in my Father's house are many rooms I go to prepare a place for you
tags: I am the way truth life
no one comes to Father except through me

text: Jesus says I am the way the truth and the life and no one comes to the Father except through me
Jesus says I am the true vine and my Father is the gardener, abide in me and I in you and you will bear much fruit, apart from me you can do nothing
On the day of Pentecost the Holy Spirit falls on the disciples like tongues of fire and they begin speaking in other languages, Peter preaches and three thousand people are saved
Saul persecutes Christians and is on his way to Damascus when a bright light blinds him and he hears the voice of Jesus asking why are you persecuting me, he is converted and becomes the apostle Paul

Paul and Silas are imprisoned in Philippi, they pray and sing hymns at midnight, an earthquake opens the prison doors, the jailer is about to kill himself but Paul stops him and leads him to salvation
tags: what must I do to be saved

text: All have sinned and fall short of the glory of God, all are justified freely by his grace through the redemption that came by Christ Jesus
God demonstrates his own love for us in this, while we were still sinners Christ died for us
The wages of sin is death but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord
There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus who walk not after the flesh but after the Spirit
All things work together for good to those who love God who are the called according to his purpose
If God is for us who can be against us, nothing can separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus, neither death nor life nor angels nor powers nor height nor depth
If you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead you will be saved
Do not be conformed to this world but be transformed by the renewing of your mind that you may prove what is the good acceptable and perfect will of God
Love is patient love is kind it does not envy or boast it is not proud rude or self-seeking it keeps no record of wrongs, love never fails
Love suffers long and is kind, love does not envy, does not parade itself, is not puffed up
We walk by faith not by sight
If anyone is in Christ he is a new creation, the old has passed away and behold the new has come
God tells Paul my grace is sufficient for you for my power is made perfect in weakness, Paul therefore boasts in his weaknesses so that the power of Christ may dwell in him
The fruit of the Spirit is love joy peace patience kindness goodness faithfulness gentleness and self-control, against such things there is no law
For it is by grace you have been saved through faith and this not of yourselves it is the gift of God not by works so that no one can boast
Put on the full armor of God so you can stand against the devil's schemes: belt of truth breastplate of righteousness feet gospel of peace shield of faith helmet of salvation sword of the Spirit
Rejoice in the Lord always and again I say rejoice
Be anxious for nothing but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God, and the peace of God will guard your hearts
I can do all things through Christ who gives me strength
God has not given us a spirit of fear but of power and of love and of a sound mind
All scripture is God-breathed and profitable for doctrine reproof correction instruction in righteousness so the man of God may be complete and equipped
The great faith chapter: faith is the substance of things hoped for the evidence of things not seen, the heroes of faith Abel Enoch Noah Abraham Moses and many others acted by faith
Faith is the substance of things hoped for the evidence of things not seen
Therefore since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses let us lay aside every weight and sin and run with endurance the race set before us looking to Jesus
Count it all joy my brothers when you meet various trials knowing that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness
Faith without works is dead, just as a body without the spirit is dead so faith without works is dead also, show me your faith by your works
Submit to God and resist the devil and he will flee from you, draw near to God and he will draw near to you
Cast all your anxiety on him because he cares for you, be sober and vigilant because your adversary the devil prowls around like a roaring lion seeking someone to devour
If we confess our sins he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness
Whoever does not love does not know God because God is love
We love because he first loved us
Behold I stand at the door and knock if anyone hears my voice and opens the door I will come in and eat with him
John sees a new heaven and a new earth and the holy city New Jerusalem coming down from God, God will wipe away every tear and there will be no more death mourning crying or pain
He himself bore our sins in his body on the cross so that we might die to sin and live to righteousness, by his wounds you have been healed
If you have faith even as small as a mustard seed you can say to this mountain move and it will move and nothing will be impossible for you
I have been crucified with Christ and I no longer live but Christ lives in me, the life I now live I live by faith in the Son of God who loved me and gave himself for me
Rejoice always pray without ceasing give thanks in all circumstances for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you
Fear not for I am with you, be not dismayed for I am your God, I will strengthen you and help you and uphold you with my righteous right hand
Be still and know that I am God I will be exalted among the nations I will be exalted in the earth
I will lift up my eyes to the hills where does my help come from, my help comes from the Lord the maker of heaven and earth, he who keeps you will not slumber
You will keep in perfect peace those whose minds are steadfast because they trust in you
Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse and see if I will not open the windows of heaven and pour out so much blessing there will not be room enough to contain it
In this world you will have tribulation but take heart I have overcome the world
God is able to do exceedingly abundantly above all that we ask or think according to the power that works in us
For nothing will be impossible with God
Go therefore and make disciples of all nations baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit
You will receive power when the Holy Spirit has come upon you and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem Judea Samaria and to the ends of the earth
For unto us a child is born unto us a Son is given and the government will be on his shoulders his name shall be called Wonderful Counselor Mighty God Everlasting Father Prince of Peace
Set your mind on things above not on things that are on earth
